title: Lindsay Frame
content:
People.ContactEmail lframe@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2022
- University of British Columbia
Practical and strategic, Lindsay helps clients manage regulatory risk—from incident response, to investigations and prosecutions, to parallel proceedings.
Lindsay is a Vancouver litigator whose practice focuses on regulatory investigations, criminal/regulatory defence, and complex disputes. She is often retained to protect clients’ interests after an environmental incident, such as a wildfire or a spill. She advises at every stage of an investigation, including on search warrants and production orders, detention of seized evidence, employee interviews, collection and preservation of evidence, and protection of constitutional rights and privilege. When matters escalate, she defends clients in criminal/regulatory prosecutions and administrative monetary penalty proceedings. She also acts in complex commercial disputes, class actions, and judicial reviews.
Lindsay often advises clients in regulated industries who face multiple parallel proceedings arising from the same event (for example, a civil class action and a criminal investigation or prosecution). Drawing on her experience in both civil and criminal litigation, she takes a coordinated, forward-looking approach to protect clients’ interests in the immediate matter, and also across all related proceedings and investigations. Lindsay is experienced at handling complex cases with voluminous documents in both civil and criminal contexts: She regularly advises on the use of e-Discovery and artificial intelligence tools, and the management of electronically stored information (ESI) and metadata.
What sets Lindsay apart is her commitment to learning and deeply understanding clients’ businesses, so she can provide practical and business-minded advice. She leverages her scientific background (B.Sc. in Integrated Sciences) to work closely with environmental experts, enabling her to understand immediate compliance issues and their root causes. She is dedicated to protecting clients’ interests and reputations when incidents occur, and to helping clients reduce future risk through robust, proactive compliance strategies.
Lindsay is a member of the firm’s national White Collar Defence and Investigations , Environmental, Regulatory, and Aboriginal , and Class Actions groups. She has appeared as counsel at all levels of court in British Columbia, the Federal Court of Canada, and the Supreme Court of Canada. Before joining McCarthy, Lindsay clerked for five justices at the Supreme Court of British Columbia.
Lindsay maintains an active pro bono practice, regularly representing marginalized clients in public interest and constitutional litigation. She is the chair of the Canadian Bar Association’s Access to Justice Committee (BC Branch).
Lindsay attended law school at UBC, where she was the recipient of a number of awards, including the Lawson Lundell LLP Prize in Constitutional Law, the Law Foundation Public Interest Award, the Harvey T. Strosberg Essay Prize in class actions, the Kenneth Douglas Landels Memorial Award, and a Top Oralist award at the Laskin Moot. Lindsay was also a member of the Law Students’ Legal Advice Program and UBC Criminal Clinic, where she provided pro bono legal services to underserved populations. Prior to law school, Lindsay obtained her Bachelor of Science (Integrated Sciences) from UBC, where she competed as a varsity athlete (UBC Thunderbirds).
